item.title: Prior, Posteriori, Derogat?; or, Ego and Ed
item.description:
Glass and peace alike betray proof of fragility under repeated blows. (David Mitchell)

Add this blog to that list; a glassy plaque of peevish suppositions of sheepish propositions of peace. Id est, don’t read this too many times, please.

One fears not the fall; but maybe the way it ends; with fatal foresight1. The trick, ergo, or rather the knack, as someone (whose works, perhaps, you didn’t like for the post hoc expressed fact that they were originally meant to be consumed at a modulation of frequencies much different than the current? My memory remains as dubious in servitude as ever) so beautifully postulated, is to simply miss hitting the surface so stoically threatening to refuse your continuity, a feat ostensibly achieved by developing an acute ignorance of the acceleration you’ve grown to assume the existence of blindly, if only because it’s always unflinchingly there. Another (whose works suffer your glares for having been quite frequently wrong, on account of their blinding blunders of biased sampling, but cannot be dismissed so easily as they professed an attempt to be less so instead of more of the opposite) reasoned – seemingly betraying a personal mistrust, that is not without empathisers, of the self’s motivations – that ‘twas in fact a fear not of getting hurled but of hurling oneself in a bout of mild curiosity. I’m not entirely sure which it is as the vertiginous incertitude of what lies downthread (granted, we are at a velocity quite terminal, given the symmetries of this scale) lends to my hesitation. But I’ll take my time, not like I’ve got a plan.

item.title: Ars(e) Gratia Artis
item.description:
The aims of art are incommensurate (as the mathematicians say) with social aims. The aim of an artist is not to solve a problem irrefutably, but to make people love life in all its countless, inexhaustible manifestations. (Leo Tolstoy)

What is art? [Term of endearment] don’t quote me, don’t quoth me, ever more. But certainly not whatever follows.

—

Space being infinite, does not give a damn. Nearly five thousand words of utter and complete balderdash (understatement), and this was the one sentence I chose to omit1, on the grounds of a comma whose presence would break the scheme, whose absence would leave its necessity under-implied, and whose parenthesisation was out of the question (aesthetic reasons, inter alia). Yay, Oxford. (The subjunctive was subjectively judged unnecessary.) The hypocrisy of not having charted the Olympians off to a similar fate has not escaped me. So a guised Apollo geysing (American pronunciation, please, just this once) up towers of steam in wrath, (however so briefly) dropping the lyre to string the bow once more, propping the liar to rethink the roles of one’s roar2; giving Æsclepius a taste of his own creation - all justified, and in part, anticipated. For far more than this obstreperous obsession obliteratively obliviating the realisation which would otherwise have obviously obviated the need for any vacillations between form and function3. (I’ll try not to do that too often. No promises.)

item.title: Ad Umbra; or, Vampire Strikes Back
item.description:
He must be theory-mad beyond redemption who, in spite of [their]: differences, shall still persist in attempting to reconcile the obstinate oils and waters of Poetry and Truth. (Edgar Allan Poe)

Roses are red Violets are blue If you thought the former green You might be deuteropic too.

I am burning glass bridges. That faint memory of coasting a caustic two,five-dichlorophenol on borosilicate a toast of catharsis. The moat-ley annulus of tranquility around this moltening fortitude an iota of solace. Opening a window. The darkness outside makes my room’s meagre scintillations reach further than I wish. Thinking of Rhapsodic openings, of introductory phrases betraying thematic portents (oh no). Of red herrings. Of this reluctant yearning for cumpescatory naps1 while these weary wares remain vainly void of those vistas of vantablack. Of salmons in no rush to d perish, keeping their monocular vigil against Platonic troglodytes; bending my knees to that nepotic nose-bloodening genuflection at the beginning of a wondrous pilgrimage. Of handing you a Brobdingnagian receptacle of words and pouring in less than a drop of meaning as I rejoice in this most Badr-ic a fashion. Aye, this nigh hill is my abode2, quite humble. Everything’s fair from the vantage of this solipsistic denial of the self.

item.title: Rebus Sic Stantibus
item.description:
I promise nothing complete, because any human thing supposed to be complete must for that very reason infallibly be faulty. (Herman Melville)

The following text is a complete feculence-fest.

This is not a blog. Or the draft of a blog. Or the unfinished husk of a dilettante impetus to exhibit profundity where none exists.

This is not a fugue-state fueled stream of unfiltered notions stemming from a feeling I’ve gaslated myself to consider worth sharing1.
